steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Predict Hotlink Origin Secret · Theory Hotlink Origin Secret
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 6992 during a AWS SDK v3 dependency canary. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to exhaustively evaluate application, WAF, origin, header, and private-error controls; do not treat TypeScript types, a resolved promise, CORS, ETag, or an emulator as stronger proof than it is; then change region, retrieval, or transfer cost and defend the revised budget action. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to exhaustively evaluate application, WAF, origin, header, and private-error controls; separate deterministic browser evidence from controlled Node and AWS transfer gates; then deliver a duplicate or reordered event and prevent duplicate stakeholder effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, treat Referer hotlink controls as a weak cost signal and use signed access or a controlled origin header only within its actual threat boundary.

For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Defend the Hotlink Origin Secret model · Theory Hotlink Origin Secret, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: Blocking unknown Referer values securely prevents content theft. Type annotations, generated SDK shapes, and resolved promises do not strengthen that runtime claim.
